metadata.title: About BAPI | BAPI
metadata.description: Learn about BAPI's 30+ year history manufacturing precision sensor solutions for building automation systems worldwide.

hero.title: Building Automation Products Inc.
hero.subtitle: Precision Sensor Solutions for Building Automation
hero.description: For over 30 years, BAPI has been a leading manufacturer of precision sensors for building automation systems, trusted by engineers worldwide.

overview.title: About BAPI
overview.paragraph1: Building Automation Products, Inc. (BAPI) was formed in 1993 from its parent company BEMCO, Inc., which was founded in 1983. Since then, BAPI has grown into a global manufacturer of precision sensors that improve building automation systems and are critical to saving energy. Every BAPI product is designed and manufactured at our headquarters in Gays Mills, Wisconsin, surrounded by the natural beauty of the rolling hills of the Driftless Region.
overview.paragraph2: At BAPI, quality and reliability are at the core of everything we do.  Our sensor products are trusted by engineers worldwide for mission-critical facilities including hospitals, data centers, clean rooms, laboratories, and commercial buildings. With over 600 product models, NIST-traceable calibration, BACnet® certification, and ISO 9001:2015 manufacturing standards, BAPI delivers precision you can depend on.

coreValues.subtitle: The principles that guide everything we do

mission.title: Mission

innovation.title: Innovation

whyBapi.title: WHY BAPI?
whyBapi.description: BAPI has been changing the way you think about sensors since 1993. We believe sensors are not just simple commodities. They are integral to a building automation controls system and critical to the mission of saving energy. Our commitment to innovation and quality has helped transform BAPI into a leading global manufacturer of sensor solutions with a tremendous focus on quality, reliability and value.

warranty.title: Warranty
warranty.description: Our products are designed and manufactured to last. We back up that claim by offering a 5-year warranty across all of our products.
warranty.footnote: A lifetime limited warranty is also available on many of our single point, room and duct room temperature sensors.*

whyBapi.bapiBackedDescription: Most sensor manufacturers will replace their defective products, but only BAPI has the confidence to go beyond the industry standard.
whyBapi.bapiBackedFootnote: 'BAPI-Backed' means we stand behind everything we sell, so be confident in the quality of our products that if one fails within the warranty period, we will not only repair or replace it, but we'll provide a product expert to offset your incurred cost.*

bapiOriginal.title: BAPI Original
bapiOriginal.description: At BAPI, we strive to be leaders in our industry by providing innovative, high quality products and services designed with you in mind.
bapiOriginal.footnote: Products displaying the "Another Original" stamp are unique to BAPI and the inspiration for these designs come from our valuable customers, talented employees and respected vendors. Those three ingredients combined create industry-leading, original solutions created to solve common HVACR problems.

metadata.title: BAPI Sensors Overview & Specifications | BAPI
metadata.description: Complete overview of BAPI sensor specifications for temperature, humidity, and pressure monitoring in building automation systems.

hero.title: BAPI Sensors Overview
hero.subtitle: Complete technical specifications for building automation sensors

introduction.heading: Temperature, Humidity & Pressure Sensor Overview
introduction.description: There are many facilities and locations today that rely on temperature, pressure and humidity sensors and transmitters to provide a stable, secure environment, such as hospitals, clean rooms and data centers. The sensor or transmitter itself can make or break the system, therefore they must be dependable, accurate and 100% compatible with the building control system. More demanding environments, advances in technology, and changing customer needs keep the industry striving for new and improved sensors and transmitters. BAPI offers a wide range of temperature, humidity and pressure sensors and transmitters in all of our room, duct, immersion and outside air units so that they are 100% compatible with the facility's control system.

thermistors.title: THERMISTORS
thermistors.description: Thermistors are thermally sensitive resistors known for exhibiting a large change in resistance with only a small change in temperature. A thermistor's change in resistance is non-linear. It follows a pre-defined curve which is provided by the thermistor manufacturer.

rtds.title: RTDs
rtds.description: RTDs (Resistance Temperature Detectors) are thermally sensitive resistive elements that exhibit a small change in resistance per degree of temperature change. RTDs are especially recognized for excellent linearity throughout their temperature range with a high degree of accuracy and repeatability.

semiconductors.title: SEMICONDUCTORS
semiconductors.description: Semiconductors are designed to exhibit a defined current output directly proportional to the absolute temperature (°K). This property makes them the most linear of all the common commercial HVAC/R sensing elements. By putting this current output across a resistor, a proportional output voltage is produced.

temperatureTransmitters.title: TEMPERATURE TRANSMITTERS
temperatureTransmitters.description: Temperature transmitters incorporate a platinum 100 Ohm or 1k Ohm RTD sensor and an amplifier. These devices provide an accurate and predictable 4 to 20mA output over a specified temperature range. They are specifically designed for temperature sensing and transmission over long distances without degradation of the 4 to 20mA signal.

humidityTransmitters.title: HUMIDITY TRANSMITTERS
humidityTransmitters.description: Humidity transmitters provide a high accuracy 4 to 20mA, 0 to 5V or 0 to 10V humidity measurement. Accuracies of 2% or 3% Relative Humidity (RH) are available. BAPI room units are protected by a molded housing with an integral filter, while duct and outside air units come with a removeable 100 micron sintered stainless steel filter. The sensor is unaffected by volatile organic compounds (VOC's) or surface contamination.

pressureTransmitters.title: PRESSURE TRANSMITTERS
pressureTransmitters.description: The heart of every BAPI Pressure Transmitter is a micro-machined, single-crystal silicon, piezoresistive pressure sensor that changes resistance as a function of applied pressure. Since silicon strain gauges have high output levels in relation to the pressure applied, the pressure levels in the BAPI diaphragm can be lower than in other non-silicon strain gauges. This means a more accurate measurement of lower pressure levels.

cta.title: Explore Our Sensor Products
cta.description: Browse our complete line of temperature, humidity, and pressure sensors

metadata.title: Technical Application Notes | BAPI
metadata.description: Explore our comprehensive library of technical application notes covering sensor installation, best practices, troubleshooting guides, and industry insights for HVAC applications.

hero.title: Application Notes
hero.subtitle: In-depth technical articles covering sensor installation, best practices, and industry insights to help you get the most out of your BAPI sensors.

valueProposition.heading: Expert Guidance from Industry Leaders
valueProposition.description: Our application notes are written by BAPI's engineering team with decades of combined experience. Each article provides practical, field-tested solutions to common challenges in sensor installation, calibration, and maintenance.

hero.title: Thermistor Overview
hero.subtitle: Thermally sensitive resistors with exceptional accuracy and reliability for HVAC applications

description.heading: Thermistor Description
description.paragraph1: BAPI Thermistors are thermally sensitive resistors known for exhibiting a large change in resistance with only a small change in temperature. It is important to note that a thermistor's change in resistance is non-linear. It follows a pre-defined curve which is provided by the thermistor manufacturer.
description.paragraph2: Thermistors are manufactured to follow a specific curve with a high degree of accuracy. All BAPI thermistors have a standard accuracy of ± 0.2 °C throughout the commercial temperature range of 0 to 70 °C. BAPI also has available a higher accuracy sensor for meeting tougher specs. The extra precision [X line has an initial accuracy of ± 0.1 °C throughout the commercial temperature range of 0 to 70 °C. Please call for availability and pricing on [X line thermistors. Both accuracy levels allow BAPI thermistors to be interchanged without the extra expense of offsetting the controller.
description.footnote: *All Passive Thermistors 10K Ω and smaller are CE compliant

interchangeability.title: Interchangeability Tolerance (Accuracy)
interchangeability.description: The maximum amount that thermistors following the same curve will differ from each other.

dissipation.title: Dissipation Constant
dissipation.description: The power needed to raise the thermistor's body temperature by 1°C. At the heart of all BAPI thermistor products is a sensor with a 2.7 mW/°C dissipation constant to ensure that self-heating stays at an absolute minimum.

stability.title: Stability (Drift)
stability.description: The amount that the resistance characteristics of a thermistor will change. BAPI uses only the highest quality, "pre-aged" thermistors with very small drift values. Over a ten year span, BAPI thermistors will not change more than 0.1°C.

operatingRange.title: Operating Range
operatingRange.description: The operating range shown is for the thermistor only. The mounting package may further limit the operating range and is described on each mounting type specification. The thermal time constant will also be affected based on the added mass of the stainless steel probe and moisture protection encapsulation.

thermalTime.title: Thermal Time Constant
thermalTime.description: Bare sensors are typically measured and specified in still air and are timed at the statistical 63.2% of the step temperature change. A stirred liquid test will typically result in a much faster response time and is also timed at 63.2% of the step temperature change. The time constant is always the same whatever the temperature step change may be.

footnotes.note1: *Other thermistors are available. Contact BAPI for availability and specifications of additional thermistor types.
footnotes.note3: Minimum quantities and long lead times may apply. The "JX5" and "5K" thermistors are typically stocked items.

outputCurve.heading: Typical Thermistor Output Curve
outputCurve.imageAlt: Typical Thermistor Output Curve 10K-2 Sensor showing resistance (Ohms) vs temperature (°C)
outputCurve.caption: Figure 1 - Typical Thermistor Output Curve 10K-2 Sensor

hero.title: RTD Overview
hero.subtitle: Resistance Temperature Detectors with excellent linearity, accuracy, and repeatability

description.heading: RTD Description
description.paragraph1: BAPI RTDs (Resistance Temperature Detectors) are thermally sensitive resistive elements that exhibit a small change in resistance per degree of temperature change. RTDs are especially recognized for excellent linearity throughout their temperature range with a high degree of accuracy and repeatability.
description.paragraph2: RTDs supplied in BAPI products feature a standard interchangeability tolerance of ±0.3°C measured at 0°C. Higher accuracy sensors are also available. The Class A line has an interchangeability tolerance of ± 0.15°C measured at 0°C. Please call for availability and pricing on Class A RTDs. Whether standard or Class A, BAPI RTDs have such a high accuracy that they can be interchanged without the expense of offsetting the controller.
description.paragraph3: Most RTD sensing elements can be packaged to withstand an extremely broad temperature range (‑200 to 600°C). For most purposes, the standard operating range should be sufficient, but we also have RTDs with a higher or lower operating temperature range. BAPI offers 1KΩ Platinum RTDs with various ranges to meet your specific application needs.

tolerance.title: Tolerance of Resistance (Accuracy)
tolerance.description: The maximum amount that RTDs following the same curve will differ from each other.

stability.title: Stability (drift)
stability.description: The amount that the RTD resistance characteristics will change. BAPI uses only the highest quality, film platinum RTD sensors with very small drift values.

operatingRange.title: Operating Range
operatingRange.description: The operating range shown is for the RTD sensor only. The mounting package may further limit the operating range and is described on each mounting type specification.

toleranceOfResistance.title: Tolerance of Resistance (Accuracy)
toleranceOfResistance.description: Range: ±0.06 of the absolute ohm value at 0°C in accordance with Standard 1/3 DIN

standardRanges.heading: Standard & Internal Temperature Ranges for the 1KΩ Platinum RTD

outputTables.heading: RTD Output Tables
outputTables.description: Detailed resistance vs. temperature tables showing precise ohm values for each RTD type across their full operating range. These tables are essential for system integration and troubleshooting.

hero.title: Semiconductor Overview
hero.subtitle: The most linear temperature sensors for commercial HVAC applications

description.heading: Semiconductor Description
description.paragraph1: BAPI semiconductors are designed to exhibit a defined current output directly proportional to the absolute temperature (°K). This property makes them the most linear of all the common commercial HVAC sensing elements. By putting this current output across a resistor, a proportional output voltage is produced.
description.paragraph2: The AD592 semiconductor temperature sensor supplied with BAPI products provides a two wire 248 to 378 micro amp output or a three wire 2.48 to 3.78 volt output over a range of -13 to 221 °F (-25 to 105 °C). These units are offset using equipment traceable to the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST). Each unit is then labeled with the actual temperature and the corresponding offset.

interchangeability.title: Interchangeability Tolerance (Accuracy)
interchangeability.description: The maximum amount that sensors following the same curve will differ from each other.

repeatability.title: Repeatability
repeatability.description: A measure of a sensor's ability to repeat the same output value for a given input value.

customOffset.heading: Custom Offset Definition and Example
customOffset.description: This is how BAPI calculates the offset value provided on the sensor label:

thermReading.title: Therm Reading
thermReading.description: The actual temperature reading according to a thermometer that is certified traceable to recognized standards by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST).

sensorReading.title: Sensor Reading
sensorReading.description: The temperature reading according to the AD592 sensor, using the output in either µA or mV and converting the output to a Fahrenheit temperature.

offset.title: Offset
offset.description: The difference between the Thermometer Reading and the Sensor Reading

customOffset.maximizeAccuracy: To maximize the sensor accuracy, simply add the offset value of the sensor reading into the controller.

example1.correction: Correction: Add (+1.6) °F to the sensor for an accurate reading: 73 + 1.6 = 74.6°F

example2.correction: Correction: Add (-1.8) °F to the sensor for an accurate reading: 77.2 + (-1.8) = 75.4°F

offsetRequirement.title: Offset Requirement
offsetRequirement.description: Required to achieve maximum accuracy. Each sensor includes a custom offset listed on each sensor.

outputCurve.heading: Typical Semiconductor Output Curve
outputCurve.alt: Typical Semiconductor Output showing Current (µA) and Voltage (V) vs Temperature
outputCurve.caption: Figure 1 - Typical Semiconductor Temperature Output

ad592Standard.heading: AD592 Standard Semiconductor Output Table
ad592Standard.description: Standard AD592 current (µA) and voltage (V) output vs temperature

ad59210K.heading: AD592-10K Semiconductor Output Table
ad59210K.description: AD592 Semiconductor with 10K shunt resistor - voltage (V) output vs temperature

cta.heading: Need Help with Semiconductor Sensors?
cta.description: Our technical team can help you understand if semiconductor sensors are right for your application.

hero.title: Pressure Transmitters
hero.subtitle: Precision silicon sensors with digital compensation for ultimate accuracy

description.heading: Pressure Sensor Description
description.paragraph1: The focal point of any sensor is the sensing element itself, and BAPI has gone to great lengths to produce one of the best sensors on the market today. The heart of every BAPI unit is a micromachined, single-crystal silicon, pressure sensor. Each sensor is fabricated using the same integrated circuit technology used to make millions of cell phones, game machines and personal computers. To control and maintain the quality of these sensors, BAPI is involved in all phases of production from design to use.
description.paragraph2: Silicon does bring with it one undesired trait —thermal sensitivity. The traditional method of compensating for this thermal sensitivity is an external circuit with discreet resistors, some of which have their own temperature dependencies, introducing more error. BAPI uses a different, unique approach. We employ a custom compensation ASIC (Application Specific Integrated Circuit) that uses digital compensation while maintaining an analog signal path, producing a sensor that is precise and interchangeable.
description.paragraph3: Because of the innovative sensor and digital temperature compensation circuit, we are able to produce a highly accurate and stable product. This accuracy is verified during final calibration at our factory using a pressure-controlled source accurate to 0.00015 inch of water and traceable to NIST standards.

accuracy.title: High Accuracy
accuracy.description: NIST-traceable accuracy of ±0.25% for most ranges

compensation.title: Digital Compensation
compensation.description: ASIC-based digital compensation eliminates thermal drift

sensor.title: Piezoresistive
sensor.description: Single-crystal silicon sensor fabricated with IC technology

outputRanges.title: Output Ranges
outputRanges.value: 4 to 20 mA, 0 to 5 V or 0 to 10V

powerConsumption.dc: 4.9 mA max DC at 0-5 VDC or 0-10 VDC Output
powerConsumption.ac: 0.12 VA max AC at 0-5 VDC or 0-10 VDC Output
powerConsumption.ma: 20 mA max, DC only at 4-20 mA Output

accuracy.title: Accuracy at 72°F (22.2°C)
accuracy.lowRangeWC: Low Range: ±0.5% of W.C. ranges 0 to 0.1", 0 to 0.25", ±0.1" and ±0.25"
accuracy.lowRangePa: Low Range: ±0.5% of Pa ranges 0 to 30, 0 to 50, ±30 and ±50 Pa
accuracy.standardHighRange: Standard and High Range: ±0.25% of range (all other ranges)

outputTables.emptyTable: Table data to be populated for this pressure range.

usingTables.heading: Using These Output Tables
usingTables.description: Select your pressure range from the tabs above to view the corresponding output values. Each table shows the relationship between pressure measurement and the three available output types: 0-5 VDC, 0-10 VDC, and 4-20 mA. Use these tables to verify sensor operation or integrate BAPI pressure transmitters into your control system.

hero.title: Humidity Transmitters
hero.subtitle: High-accuracy 4-20mA, 0-5V, and 0-10V humidity transmitters for demanding applications

description.heading: Humidity Transmitter Description
description.paragraph1: BAPI humidity transmitters provide a high accuracy 4 to 20mA, 0 to 5V or 0 to 10V humidity measurement. Accuracies of 2% RH are available. Duct and outside air units come with a removable sintered stainless steel filter. On duct and outside air units, the filter may be cleaned with warm, distilled water.
description.paragraph2: These units are microprocessor based and do not require any field calibration.
description.paragraph3: For all non-room configurations, BAPI humidity transmitters come standard in a ruggedized package. Ruggedized transmitters are suitable for locations where moisture or condensation may be a problem. The potting material used to ruggedize the transmitters has a high thermal conductivity to eliminate circuit overheating and allow thermal expansion to minimize the stress on the circuit components. Due to the extremely low moisture absorption properties of the epoxy, a ruggedized transmitter will remain operational even if temporarily immersed in water.
description.paragraph4: Many tests and studies have been conducted on the sensor incorporated into these humidity transmitters to assure that they provide longterm accuracy and durability. For applications requiring even higher accuracy, however, certified units are available which have been tested and offset against an NIST traceable reference. Please call for details or with specific requirements.

accuracy.title: High Accuracy
accuracy.description: Precision humidity measurement for critical applications

durability.title: Durable Design
durability.description: Operational even if temporarily immersed in water

outputRanges.title: Output Ranges
outputRanges.value: 4 to 20 mA, 0 to 5 V, or 0 to 10 V

powerConsumption.title: Power Consumption
powerConsumption.dc: DC: 22 mA max (0-5 VDC or 4-20 mA), 6 mA max (0-10 VDC)
powerConsumption.ac: AC: 0.53 VA max (0-5 VDC or 4-20 mA), 0.14 VA max (0-10 VDC)

responseTime.title: Response Time
responseTime.note: in moving air for a 63% step

cta.heading: Need a Humidity Transmitter?
cta.description: Our humidity transmitters provide accurate, long-term reliable measurement with minimal drift.

hero.title: Temperature Transmitters
hero.subtitle: Precision 4-20mA transmitters for accurate temperature sensing over long distances

description.heading: Temperature Transmitter Description
description.paragraph1: BAPI's temperature transmitters incorporate a 1KΩ RTD sensor and an amplifier. These devices provide an accurate two wire, 4 to 20mA output over a specified temp range. They are specifically designed for long distance transmission over long distances without degradation of the 4 to 20mA signal. 100Ω units also available upon request.
description.paragraph2: Each temperature transmitter is not configurable for its specified temp range. If you don't offer the right transmitter at the field or the light transmitter in Class A RTD's to improve overall accuracy. The specified unit at the calibrated range offers better accuracy to minimize the cross on the control components. Use the accuracy of the matched pair unit becomes a function of the transfer linearity (RTD curve) and reference transmitter somewhat.
description.paragraph3: Standard units will offset 0–100°F ( commonly found ) - Reference Thermometer (example) :
description.paragraph6: These accuracies are for the worst range of the sensor, although the accuracies in the midpoint of the sensor will be tighter. Run these over your BAPI representative for details.
description.paragraph7: BAPI temperature transmitters come in a ruggedized package for all non-room configurations where moisture or condensation may be a problem. The potting material used to ruggedize the transmitters has a high thermal conductivity to eliminate circuit overheating and allow thermal expansion to minimize the stress on the control components. Due to the extremely low moisture absorption properties of the epoxy, a ruggedized transmitter will remain operational even if temporarily immersed in water.

output.title: Output
output.value: 4 to 20 mA, 0 to 5V, 1 to 5V, 0 to 10V, 2 to 10V

connections.title: Connections
connections.value: Four 22-gauge etched Teflon leads or terminal blocks

fieldAdjustments.heading: Field Adjustments
fieldAdjustments.description: Each transmitter is calibrated to the specified temperature range that is ordered. Field adjustments are not available. If adjustments are needed, please contact your BAPI representative.

cta.heading: Need a Temperature Transmitter?
cta.description: Our T1K transmitters provide accurate 4-20mA signals for long-distance temperature measurement applications.
